title: (BUG) cli: init() has TOCTOU race on keyfile existence check
description: In cli/src/commands.rs init() (line 129), the keyfile existence check is: if path.exists() { bail\!(...) }. Between the exists() check and write_secret_file() creating the file, a concurrent second 'nie init' invocation (or a race with another process) could see the file as absent and proceed. Both then call write_secret_file() with OpenOptions create(true) truncate(true) — the second writer overwrites the first, silently destroying the first-generated key. Fix: use OpenOptions::create_new(true) (fails if file already exists, atomic on POSIX) inside write_secret_file instead of a separate exists() check. This is a low-severity race (requires two concurrent init invocations) but the consequence is silent key destruction.
